para_trans: Regarding transfers in the cadre of Deputy Superintendent Land Records (Group-B Gazetted).
Government of Maharashtra
Revenue and Forest Department
GOVERNMENT ORDER, NUMBER: TRANSFER-0426 / QUESTION 137 / AASTA-6
Madam Kama Marg, Martyr Rajguru Chowk,
Mantralaya, Mumbai - 400 032,
Date: June 22, 2026
Government Orders: -
The Maharashtra Regulation and Prohibition of Transfer of Government Employees Act, 2005.

As per the approval given by the competent authority under the provisions of Rules 4 (4) and 4 (4), the Deputy Superintendent, for the convenience of the Administrator, shall appoint the following officers of the Land Records (Group-B Gazetted) cadre as the Deputy Superintendent No. There are 4 here.
The following positions are being transferred: -
(2) The said order comes into force with immediate effect and the aforesaid officer is hereby transferred from the post he is holding to join the post indicated in the order with immediate effect.
Unilateral work is being done. The concerned officer shall preside over the office of his office.
It is necessary to join in time.

3. The Commissioner of Deposits and the Director of Land Records (MDLR) shall be informed of the date on which the officer concerned joined the place of new posting. State), Pune should immediately inform the Government on the e-mail id doe6.rfd @ maharashtra.gov.in.
4. Leave of the officer concerned shall not be granted for any reason except with the previous sanction of the Government.

Also, in case of receipt of applications for non-acceptance of any leave applications of officers transferred by the original office as per Rule-30 (c) of the MNS (Leave) Rules, 1981, the same shall be deemed to be relevant.

The officer must return the registration to the original address and the payment by post. If applications for leave are sent by officers transferred to the parent office, these applications will not be considered.

5. In the event of non-joining of the place of new posting immediately in accordance with the said transfer order or any attempt to cancel the transfer under any kind of pressure, the said action shall be governed by the Maharashtra Civil Services (Conduct) Rules.
table_trans: Deputy Superintendent of Land Records, Ashti,
the Deputy Superintendent of Land Records,

para_trans: Being in violation of Rule 23 of 1979, it is considered misconduct against them.
Those concerned should note that disciplinary action will be taken.
The Government Order is available on the Government of Maharashtra website www.maharashtra.gov.in.
